html:
[1411]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 然则 - A classical connective meaning 'thus' or 'therefore,' used to introduce a conclusion or a new point.
  • 抚朱弦，吹玉管，骋纤腰，矜皓齿 - Phrases describing various forms of entertainment and display: playing a red-colored stringed instrument, blowing a jade flute, showing off a slender waist, and flaunting white teeth.
  • 皆冶容之末事 - Means 'all are trivial matters of adornment.'
  • 是不为也 - Means 'these are not what I do.'
  • 昨闻足下有幽隐之志 - Means 'I heard yesterday that you have a reclusive intention.'
  • 愿一款颜 - Means 'I wish to meet you briefly.'
  • 非敢自献 - Means 'I do not dare to offer myself.'
  • 宵清月朗 - Means 'a clear night with bright moon.'
  • 喜觌良人 - Means 'to see a good person with pleasure.'
  • 桑中之讥 - Refers to the 'plum-blossom grove' (sangzhong) allusion, which is a euphemism for illicit love affairs.
  • 亦不能耻 - Means 'I cannot be ashamed of it.'
  • 倘运与时会 - Means 'if fortune aligns with the times.'
  • 少承周旋 - Means 'to receive a little favor or attention.'
  • 必无累于盛德 - Means 'it will not tarnish your great virtue.'
  • 祯拜而纳之 - Means 'Zhen bowed and accepted it.'
  • 唯霾晦不复至 - Means 'only on overcast and dark days does she not come.'
  • 常遇风雨 - Means 'often encountering wind and rain.'
  • 欲止之 - Means 'to want to stop her.'
  • 答曰 - Means 'she replied.'
  • 公违晨夕之养 - Means 'you violate the morning and evening care.'
  • 就岩壑而居 - Means 'to live in the mountains and valleys.'
  • 得非求理静业乎 - Means 'is this not to seek truth and tranquility?'
  • 奈何欲使采过之人 - Means 'why do you want to make someone who has passed away.'
  • 称君违亲而就偶 - Means 'to call you abandoning your parents for a companion.'
  • 非但损公盛名 - Means 'not only will it damage your great reputation.'
  • 亦当速某之生命耳 - Means 'it will also hasten my life.'
  • 后半年 - Means 'after half a year.'
  • 家僮归告祯乳母 - Means 'a servant returned to tell Zhen's wet nurse.'
  • 母乃潜伏佛榻以观之 - Means 'the mother then secretly hid on the Buddha altar to observe.'
  • 果自隙而出 - Means 'indeed, she came out from a crack.'
  • 入西幢 - Means 'into the western tent.'
  • 澄澄一灯耳 - Means 'a clear and bright lamp.'
  • 因扑灭之 - Means 'she then extinguished it.'
  • 后遂绝红裳者 - Means 'afterward, she no longer appeared as a red-clad woman.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 抚朱弦，吹玉管 - Describes playing a stringed instrument and blowing a flute, common forms of entertainment in ancient China.
  • 骋纤腰，矜皓齿 - Refers to the display of physical beauty, a common theme in classical literature.
  • 桑中之讥 - Refers to the 'plum-blossom grove' allusion, which is a euphemism for illicit love affairs.
  • 岩壑 - Refers to mountains and valleys, often associated with reclusive living in Chinese culture.
  • 佛榻 - Refers to a Buddha altar, a place of religious significance.
  • 红裳者 - Refers to a red-clad woman, likely a supernatural or divine figure.

Proper Nouns

  • 祯 - The name of the protagonist, Zhen.
  • 桑中之讥 - A reference to a well-known allusion in Chinese literature.

[1412]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 石妖 - Literally 'stone demon,' a term used to describe a supernatural or uncanny phenomenon associated with the stone.
  • 結社 - Means 'to form a society or association,' often for scholarly or literary purposes.
  • 牆側 - Refers to 'the side of the wall,' indicating a specific location.
  • 搗衣石 - Literally 'washing clothes stone,' a type of stone used for pounding clothes to remove dirt.
  • 潔白潤膩 - Describes the stone as 'pure white, smooth, and lustrous.'
  • 裸裎 - Means 'to be naked or unclothed,' often in a casual or relaxed manner.
  • 绸缪 - Refers to 'intimate or close contact,' often in a romantic or sexual context.
  • 欻然 - Describes something happening suddenly or unexpectedly.
  • 劅掘 - Means 'to dig or excavate,' often with a sense of uncovering or uncovering something hidden.
  • 入地 - Means 'to penetrate the ground,' indicating depth.
  • 取火焚之 - Means 'to take fire and burn it,' indicating the act of setting something on fire.
  • 血出如濡 - Describes blood flowing as if it were wet or fresh.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 武林 - Refers to 'Wulin,' an area in Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province, known for its historical significance and cultural heritage.
  • 諸子結社 - Refers to a group of scholars or intellectuals forming an association, often for literary or scholarly pursuits.
  • 搗衣石 - A common feature in traditional Chinese settings, used for washing clothes by pounding them.
  • 青衣女子 - Refers to a woman dressed in a simple, plain dress, often associated with servants or lower-class women.
  • 劍痕 - Refers to 'sword marks,' indicating the presence of cuts or scratches made by a sword.

Proper Nouns

  • 武林 - Wulin, an area in Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province.
  • 張生 - Zhang, a character in the story.

[1413]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 先年武林有少年结伴看春 - Indicates a group of young men from Wulin (Hangzhou) who went out to enjoy the spring scenery.
  • 按察司前，久立稠众之中 - "按察司" refers to the office of the censor, and "稠众" means a crowded place.
  • 其下偶停一空担 - "空担" means an empty bamboo basket or tray.
  • 担中有一白石子，腻泽可爱 - "腻泽" describes something smooth and lustrous.
  • 疑是压秤物也 - "压秤物" refers to weights used in scales.
  • 少年不觉摩娑入袖 - "摩娑" means to stroke or caress.
  • 夜归，取纳床头 - "纳" means to place or put.
  • 忽见一碧衣女子，映月而至 - "碧衣" means a green or blue robe, and "映月" means under the moonlight.
  • 就之求合 - "求合" means to seek union or intimacy.
  • 扪其体如冰，固叩无语 - "扪其体如冰" means touching her body felt like ice, and "固叩无语" means she remained silent despite being asked.
  • 少年惧是鬼物，急取火视之，忽不见矣 - "鬼物" means a ghost or supernatural being, and "急取火视之" means quickly lit a fire to look at her.
  • 明夕复至，拒之如初 - "明夕" means the next night, and "拒之如初" means she rejected him again.
  • 众咸谓此石为祟 - "祟" means a malevolent spirit or omen.
  • 乃移至他室，遂绝 - "移至他室" means moved it to another room, and "遂绝" means it stopped.
  • 后遇玉工出示，剖之，得白璧焉 - "玉工" means a jade craftsman, and "白璧" means a white jade disc.
  • 质色非常，因获厚镪 - "质色非常" means the quality and color were extraordinary, and "厚镪" means a large sum of money.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 武林 - Refers to Wulin, an ancient name for Hangzhou.
  • 按察司 - The office of the censor, responsible for administrative oversight and judicial functions.
  • 碧衣女子 - A woman dressed in green or blue, often associated with supernatural beings in folklore.
  • 白石子 - A smooth, lustrous stone, possibly a jade-like material.
  • 白璧 - A white jade disc, symbolizing purity and value.
  • 厚镪 - A large sum of money, indicating significant wealth.
  • 《狯园》 - A collection of stories and anecdotes, possibly a compendium of strange tales.

Proper Nouns

  • 武林 - Wulin (Hangzhou)
  • 按察司 - Office of the Censor
  • 碧衣女子 - Green-robed woman
  • 白石子 - Smooth, lustrous stone
  • 白璧 - White jade disc
  • 厚镪 - Large sum of money
  • 《狯园》 - Collection of stories and anecdotes

[1414]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 尝见一书载 - Once I saw a book that recorded; indicates a reference to a specific source.
  • 至夜化成女子 - At night it transformed into a woman; describes a supernatural event.
  • 则妇人为石，石为妇人，无不有矣 - So women turning into stones, stones turning into women, such things are not uncommon; emphasizes the frequency of such transformations.
  • 嬉游湖上者竞买泥孩 - Those who played on the lake competed to buy clay children; describes a popular activity.
  • 名曰湖上土宜 - Called the local specialty of the lake; gives a name to the practice.
  • 忽闻有人歌诗云 - Suddenly heard someone singing a poem saying; introduces a supernatural event.
  • 绣被长年劳辗转，香帏还许暂相偎 - The embroidered quilt has been tossing and turning for years, the fragrant curtain still allows a brief embrace; a poetic expression.
  • 遂与合焉 - And they consummated their union; describes a supernatural event.
  • 知此为怪 - Realized that this was a supernatural event; indicates a conclusion.
  • 其怪遂绝 - The supernatural event ceased; indicates the end of the phenomenon.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 阳羡 - A county in present-day Jiangsu Province, known for its cultural and historical significance.
  • 临安 - The capital of the Southern Song Dynasty, located in present-day Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province.
  • 宋时 - Refers to the Song Dynasty, a period of significant cultural and technological development in Chinese history.
  • 泥孩 - Clay figures, often used in folk traditions and superstitions.
  • 湖上土宜 - A local specialty or custom related to the lake, reflecting regional practices.
  • 象院 - A place name, possibly referring to a temple or a significant location in the story.
  • 《夷坚续志》 - A collection of supernatural stories and legends from the Song Dynasty, compiled by Lin Shu.

Proper Nouns

  • 阳羡 - Yang County, a place name.
  • 临安 - Lin'an, the capital of the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 泥孩 - Clay children, a specific type of object.
  • 象院 - A place name, possibly a temple or a significant location.
  • 《夷坚续志》 - The *Yijian Xuzhi*, a collection of supernatural stories and legends.

[1415]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 石狮 - Refers to a stone lion statue, often placed outside buildings for protection.
  • 郭外 - Means 'outside the city walls,' indicating a location beyond the urban area.
  • 择婿欲嫁 - Means 'choosing a husband and wanting to marry,' describing the intention of a family to arrange a marriage.
  • 妖祟所惑 - Means 'bewitched by demons or evil spirits,' indicating that the daughter is under the influence of supernatural forces.
  • 斋醮符法 - Refers to rituals involving incense, offerings, and magical symbols, often used in Taoist practices to ward off evil.
  • 禳治 - Means 'to exorcise or treat,' referring to the efforts to remove the evil influence.
  • 靡不至 - Means 'nothing was left untried,' emphasizing the extensive measures taken.
  • 经年弗痊 - Means 'for many years without recovery,' indicating the prolonged duration of the problem.
  • 夜闻女歌呼笑语 - Means 'heard the daughter singing, calling, and laughing at night,' describing the strange behavior of the daughter.
  • 密往窥之 - Means 'secretly went to look,' indicating the cautious approach of the observer.
  • 高而且大 - Means 'tall and large,' describing the size of the stone lion.
  • 蹑其背而立 - Means 'climbed onto its back and stood,' describing the action of the observer.
  • 元不干张秀才事 - Means 'this has nothing to do with Scholar Zhang,' indicating that the stone lion is the cause of the problem.
  • 愕然 - Means 'startled,' describing the reaction of the observer.
  • 知必此物为怪 - Means 'knew that this object must be the cause of the problem,' indicating the realization of the observer.
  • 将以明日告陈 - Means 'planned to inform Chen the next day,' indicating the intention to take action.
  • 陈氏谓张有道术 - Means 'Chen's family believed that Zhang had magical abilities,' indicating the trust placed in Zhang.
  • 清旦 - Means 'early morning,' indicating the time when Zhang was invited.
  • 邀致人视 - Means 'invited someone to come and look,' indicating the formal request for help.
  • 张不言昨夕事 - Means 'Zhang did not mention what happened last night,' indicating his discretion.
  • 但诵乾元亨利贞 - Means 'only recited the phrase 'Qian, Yuan, Heng, Li, Zhen,' referring to the opening words of the *I Ching* (Book of Changes).
  • 吾用圣人之经以临邪孽 - Means 'I use the teachings of the sages to confront evil spirits,' indicating the use of classical texts for spiritual protection.
  • 如将汤沃残雪耳 - Means 'it is like pouring hot water over melting snow,' a metaphor for the ease with which the evil is dispelled.
  • 形模狞恶 - Means 'shape and appearance are fierce and ugly,' describing the appearance of the stone lion.
  • 宜亟去之 - Means 'it should be removed immediately,' indicating the urgency of the action.
  • 陈即呼匠凿碎 - Means 'Chen immediately called a craftsman to break it,' describing the immediate action taken.
  • 辇而投诸水 - Means 'carried it to the water and threw it in,' indicating the disposal of the stone lion.
  • 女遂平安 - Means 'the daughter then became well,' indicating the resolution of the problem.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 金华县 - Refers to Jinhua County, a place in Zhejiang Province, China.
  • 石狮 - Stone lions are common in Chinese architecture, often placed at the entrances of temples, palaces, and homes for protection.
  • 斋醮符法 - These are traditional Taoist rituals and practices, often involving the use of talismans and incantations to protect against evil.
  • 乾元亨利贞 - These are the first five words of the *I Ching* (Book of Changes), a classic Chinese text used for divination and wisdom.
  • 形模狞恶 - The description of the stone lion as fierce and ugly reflects the belief that such statues can be powerful and potentially malevolent if not properly managed.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 石狮，高而且大 - This phrase describes the size of the stone lion, which is crucial to understanding its significance in the story.
  • 元不干张秀才事，何为苦我？ - This rhetorical question emphasizes the stone lion's role in the problem.
  • 张不言昨夕事，但诵乾元亨利贞 - This sequence shows Zhang's cautious and methodical approach to solving the problem.
  • 吾用圣人之经以临邪孽，如将汤沃残雪耳 - This metaphorical statement explains the effectiveness of the classical text in dispelling the evil.
  • 宜亟去之 - This imperative sentence indicates the urgency of the action to remove the stone lion.

Proper Nouns

  • 金华县 - Jinhua County.
  • 陈秀才 - Scholar Chen.
  • 张生 - Scholar Zhang.
  • 乾元亨利贞 - The opening words of the *I Ching* (Book of Changes).

[1416]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 葺旧庐为书舍 - Repair an old house and turn it into a study.
  • 疏渠引泉 - Dredge channels and draw water from springs.
  • 五色交辉 - Various colors interweave or blend harmoniously.
  • 青林翠竹 - Green trees and emerald bamboo.
  • 四时具备 - All four seasons are present, indicating year-round beauty.
  • 晓雾将歇 - Morning mist is about to dissipate.
  • 猿鸟和鸣 - Monkeys and birds sing in harmony.
  • 夕日欲颓 - Sun is setting.
  • 沉鳞竞跃 - Fish leap out of the water, often used to describe aquatic life during sunset.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 黎阳 - Historical place name in Henan Province, known for its cultural significance.
  • 儒生 - Refers to a scholar or a Confucian student.
  • 书舍 - A study or a place for learning.
  • 高韵 - Term used to describe a person's noble character or high spirits.
  • 请益 - Term used in classical Chinese to describe the act of seeking further knowledge or instruction.
  • 大悦 - Term used to describe a person's great pleasure or delight.
  • 携手而入 - Term used to describe a romantic or intimate act of entering a place together.
  • 并肩而坐 - Term used to describe a romantic or intimate act of sitting side by side.
  • 献一诗 - Term used to describe the act of presenting a poem.
  • 善 - Affirmative response used in classical Chinese to indicate agreement or approval.

Proper Nouns

  • 黎阳 - Historical place name in Henan Province.
  • 廷肃 - Courtesy name of the scholar.
  • 美女子 - Beautiful woman.
  • 邻家女 - Woman from a neighboring house.
  • 高韵 - Term used to describe a person's noble character or high spirits.
  • 请益 - Term used in classical Chinese to describe the act of seeking further knowledge or instruction.
  • 大悦 - Term used to describe a person's great pleasure or delight.
  • 携手而入 - Term used to describe a romantic or intimate act of entering a place together.
  • 并肩而坐 - Term used to describe a romantic or intimate act of sitting side by side.
  • 献一诗 - Term used to describe the act of presenting a poem.
  • 善 - Affirmative response used in classical Chinese to indicate agreement or approval.

[1417]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 霜冷秋高白帝城 - Poetic description of a cold, high autumn night in White Emperor City, combining natural imagery with a sense of place.
  • 闺中力尽恨难平 - Phrase expressing the exhaustion and unresolved sorrow of a woman in her chamber.
  • 西风庭院叮当响 - Describes the sound of the west wind in the courtyard, using onomatopoeia to convey a sense of desolation.
  • 晓夜楼台断续声 - Refers to the intermittent sounds heard from the towers at dawn and dusk, symbolizing longing or unrest.
  • 捣碎乡心愁欲结 - Metaphorical expression where '乡心' (homesickness) is 'dashed' or 'crushed,' intensifying the sorrow.
  • 惊回客枕梦难成 - Describes the difficulty of falling asleep due to the disturbance of a traveler's dreams.
  • 惟虑不入笙歌耳 - Expresses the worry that one's sorrow might not be heard amidst the sounds of music and song.
  • 窗恼玉关无限情 - Phrase suggesting that the window (or perhaps the wind) is filled with endless emotions related to the '玉关' (Jade Gate), a symbol of distant places and longing.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 白帝城 - Refers to White Emperor City, a historical and poetic location in China, often associated with autumn and melancholy.
  • 玉关 - Refers to the Jade Gate, a historical border pass in the northwest, symbolizing distant places and the longing for loved ones.
  • 纲 - Likely a character name, possibly referring to a man in a literary or historical context.
  • 占娘 - A female character name, possibly from a literary or historical narrative.

Proper Nouns

  • 白帝城 - White Emperor City.
  • 玉关 - Jade Gate.
  • 纲 - A character name.
  • 占娘 - A character name.

[1418]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 牛骨等物 - Literally 'ox bones and other things,' likely referring to items of value or specific objects.
  • 不事系泗州獄 - 'Not involved in the case at Sizhou prison,' where '不事' means not being implicated or involved.
  • 周旋拔挈出獄 - 'Helped and escorted out of prison,' where '周旋' means to help or assist, and '拔挈' means to escort or lead out.
  • 話別 - 'Farewell conversation,' indicating a parting talk.
  • 忽有一人問翁姓名 - 'Suddenly, a person asked the old man's name,' where '忽' means suddenly.
  • 即下拜 - 'Immediately bowed,' indicating a respectful gesture.
  • 家有一女，為魅所挠 - 'Has a daughter who is troubled by a spirit,' where '為魅所挠' means 'troubled by a spirit.'
  • 祛之不动 - 'Could not drive it away,' where '祛' means to drive away or dispel.
  • 只畏泗州王某耳 - 'Only fears Mr. Wang of Sizhou,' indicating a specific fear.
  • 一路訪公行止 - 'Traveled along the way to find out about your movements,' where '行止' means movements or actions.
  • 特此懇告 - 'Specially comes to beg for help,' indicating a sincere request.
  • 勿惮百里之遠 - 'Do not hesitate to travel a hundred miles,' where '勿惮' means do not hesitate.
  • 救女生全 - 'To save the girl and make her whole,' indicating a complete recovery.
  • 當不靳千金之報 - 'Will not be stingy with a thousand gold pieces as a reward,' where '不靳' means not stingy.
  • 我實無他伎倆 - 'I really have no special skills,' indicating a lack of expertise.
  • 豈堪治怪 - 'How can I handle such a strange case,' where '堪' means to be able to.
  • 請不已 - 'Kept asking,' indicating persistence.
  • 向年自鳳陽還泗 - 'Last year, returning from Fuyang to Sizhou,' where '向年' means last year.
  • 乘一驢，復挈一空驢行 - 'Riding one donkey and carrying another empty donkey,' where '挈' means to carry or lead.
  • 見一道人襥被而步 - 'Saw a Taoist monk with a bundle of bedding walking,' where '襥被' means a bundle of bedding.
  • 乏錢 - 'Lacks money,' indicating poverty.
  • 吾以空驢借之 - 'I lent him the empty donkey,' indicating an act of kindness.
  • 感荷 - 'Grateful,' indicating a sense of gratitude.
  • 以一卷書授我 - 'Gave me a scroll of writing,' indicating a gift.
  • 依此而行，可斷百怪 - 'Following this will be able to dispel a hundred strange cases,' where '斷' means to dispel or resolve.
  • 然勿受人酬謝世 - 'But do not accept any reward from people,' indicating a condition.
  • 慢置書於笥 - 'Carelessly placed the book in a box,' where '慢' means carelessly.
  • 爾家怪所畏見者 - 'The thing in your family that is afraid to see,' indicating a specific fear.
  • 歸覓書 - 'Returned to find the book,' indicating a search.
  • 令其人先還 - 'Told the person to return first,' indicating a sequence of actions.
  • 具瓮一口，方砖一块，血狗皮一张，炽炭以待 - 'Prepare a jar, a square brick, a piece of blood-stained dog skin, and hot charcoal,' indicating specific items needed.
  • 且宜戒言 - 'And should refrain from speaking,' indicating a warning.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 淮人 - Refers to a person from the Huai River region, which was historically significant in China.
  • 泗州 - Refers to Sizhou, an ancient city in the Huai River region.
  • 王翁 - Refers to an old man named Wang, a common name in Chinese history.
  • 魅 - Refers to a spirit or ghost, often associated with supernatural phenomena.
  • 鳳陽 - Refers to Fuyang, a city in Anhui Province, known for its historical significance.
  • 道人 - Refers to a Taoist monk, indicating a religious figure.
  • 襥被 - Refers to a bundle of bedding, a common item for travelers.
  • 千金 - Refers to a large sum of money, indicating a significant reward.
  • 笥 - Refers to a box or container, often used to store items.
  • 血狗皮 - Refers to a piece of blood-stained dog skin, often used in traditional rituals.
  • 炽炭 - Refers to hot charcoal, often used in traditional practices.

Proper Nouns

  • 劉還 - The name of the person from Huai.
  • 王翁 - The name of the old man.
  • 泗州 - The name of the city.
  • 鳳陽 - The name of the city.
  • 道人 - The title of the Taoist monk.
  • 書 - The name of the scroll given by the Taoist monk.
  • 瓮 - The name of the jar.
  • 方砖 - The name of the square brick.
  • 血狗皮 - The name of the piece of blood-stained dog skin.
  • 炽炭 - The name of the hot charcoal.

[1419]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 次日 - The next day, a straightforward temporal expression.
  • 翁乃赍符剑以往 - The old man then took the talisman and sword with him. '赍' (to carry) and '以往' (to go) are combined to indicate the action of taking something along for a journey.
  • 入门，怪即言于室曰 - Entering the door, the strange creature immediately spoke to the room saying. '怪' (strange creature) is a general term for an unusual or supernatural being.
  • 吾当敛避 - I should withdraw. This is a polite way of saying 'I should leave' or 'I should hide.'
  • 方欲出而王翁已入 - Just as it was about to leave, the Master Wang had already entered. '方欲' (just about to) and '已入' (had already entered) create a contrast in timing.
  • 大叱曰 - Shouted loudly. '大叱' (shouted loudly) emphasizes the intensity of the shout.
  • 死老魅何之 - Where is the old demon going? '死老魅' (old demon) is a derogatory term for a malevolent spirit.
  • 躅蹐谓女曰 - Hesitantly told the woman. '躅蹐' (hesitantly) describes a cautious or fearful manner.
  • 何处可逃 - Where can I escape? This is a direct question expressing fear.
  • 此中可 - This place is okay. '此中' (this place) and '可' (okay) are used to indicate a suitable location.
  • 翁以狗皮封之 - The old man sealed it with dog skin. '封之' (sealed it) is a common phrase for sealing something.

  • 法师舍我，我有妻妹可怜 - Please spare me, I have a wife and sister-in-law who are pitiful. '法师' (master) is a respectful term for a spiritual practitioner.
  • 翁问 - The old man asked. '翁' (old man) is a respectful term for an elder.
  • 尔何妖 - What kind of demon are you? '何妖' (what kind of demon) is a direct question.
  • 答曰 - Answered. '答曰' (answered) is a common phrase for indicating a response.
  • 丑氏 - Chou clan. '丑氏' (Chou clan) is a specific reference to a family or lineage.
  • 牛而曰丑者，讳之也 - Because it is a cow and says 'Chou,' it is being cautious about its name. '讳之' (being cautious about its name) suggests a cultural or personal reason for avoiding a certain name.
  • 促令供状 - Urged to make a confession. '促令' (urged) indicates a strong request.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 僧体枯瘦，气息恹然，渐无生气 - Describes the monk's emaciated appearance and lack of vitality, with '气息恹然' meaning 'weak and listless breathing' and '渐无生气' indicating a gradual decline.
  • 百端罔效 - Means 'all efforts were in vain,' emphasizing the futility of attempts to treat the monk's condition.
  • 察汝病脉，痨瘵兼攻，阴邪甚盛 - The old monk examines the monk's pulse, diagnosing a combination of consumption (痨瘵) and strong evil qi (阴邪).
  • 必有所致，苟不明言，事无济矣 - Suggests that the illness must have a specific cause, and if it is not clearly stated, there will be no benefit.
  • 湛然骇惧，勉述往事 - '湛然' is the name of the monk, '骇惧' means 'frightened and fearful,' and '勉述往事' means 'reluctantly recounted past events.'
  • 是矣。然此祟不除，则汝恙不愈 - '是矣' means 'that's it,' and '此祟不除' means 'this evil spirit is not dispelled,' indicating that the illness will not be cured.
  • 伺其往而踪迹之，则治术可施也 - '伺其往而踪迹之' means 'wait for her to leave and follow her,' and '治术可施也' means 'then the healing method can be applied.'
  • 密以一物置其身 - Means 'secretly place something on her body.'
  • 吾辈避于房外，俟临别时击门为约 - '吾辈' means 'we,' '避于房外' means 'hide outside the room,' '俟临别时' means 'wait until the moment of parting,' and '击门为约' means 'knock on the door as a signal.'
  • 吾辈协力追尾，必得所至，则祟可破矣 - '吾辈协力追尾' means 'we will work together to follow her,' and '必得所至' means 'we will surely find out where she goes,' indicating that the evil spirit can be dispelled.

  • 祟 - Refers to an evil spirit or ghost, often associated with supernatural causes of illness.
  • 击门为约 - A traditional method of signaling or making an agreement, often used in secretive or ritualistic contexts.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence describes the monk's condition in a single, flowing statement, with '气息恹然' and '渐无生气' emphasizing the gradual decline.
  • The old monk's diagnosis is presented as a single, complex statement, with '痨瘵兼攻' and '阴邪甚盛' indicating the severity of the illness.
  • This sentence presents a conditional argument, with '必有所致' and '事无济矣' forming a logical conclusion.
  • The monk's emotional state and action are linked in a single clause, with '骇惧' and '勉述' showing his fear and reluctance.
  • This sentence presents a conditional plan, with '伺其往而踪迹之' and '治术可施也' forming a logical sequence.

  • 后数月，哀帝崩 - Literal translation: 'after several months, Emperor Ai died.' '后数月' means 'after several months,' '哀帝崩' means 'Emperor Ai died.' This phrase indicates that Emperor Ai died after several months.
  • 太皇太后召大司马贤，引见东厢 - Literal translation: 'the Grand Empress Dowager summoned the Grand Marshal Xian and introduced him to the eastern hall.' '太皇太后' means 'Grand Empress Dowager,' '召' means 'summoned,' '大司马贤' is likely a title and name, '引见' means 'introduced,' '东厢' means 'eastern hall.' This phrase means 'the Grand Empress Dowager summoned the Grand Marshal Xian and introduced him to the eastern hall.'
  • 问以丧事调度 - Literal translation: 'asked about the funeral arrangements.' '问' means 'asked,' '以' means 'about,' '丧事' means 'funeral arrangements,' '调度' means 'arrangements' or 'management.' This phrase means 'asked about the funeral arrangements.'
  • 贤内忧，不能对，免冠谢 - Literal translation: 'Xian was internally worried, could not answer, and apologized by removing his hat.' '贤内忧' means 'Xian was internally worried,' '不能对' means 'could not answer,' '免冠谢' means 'removed his hat and apologized.' This phrase indicates that Xian was worried, could not answer, and apologized by removing his hat.

  • 久之，延年弟季与中人乱 - After a long time, Ruan Yan's younger brother, Ji, had an affair with a eunuch. '久之' means after a long time, '延年弟季' means Ruan Yan's younger brother Ji, and '与中人乱' means to have an affair with a eunuch.
  • 及李夫人卒后，其爱驰 - After Lady Li died, his favor declined. '及' means after, '李夫人卒后' means after Lady Li's death, '其爱驰' means his favor declined.
  • 上遂诛延年兄弟宗族 - The emperor then executed Ruan Yan and his family. '上' means the emperor, '遂' means then, '诛' means to execute, and '延年兄弟宗族' means Ruan Yan and his family.
  • 是后宠臣，大底外戚之家也 - After this, the emperor's favorites were mostly from the imperial family. '是后' means after this, '宠臣' means favorites, '大底' means mostly, and '外戚之家' means the imperial family.
  • 卫青、霍去病皆爱幸，然亦以功能自进 - Wei Qing and Huo Qubing were also favored, but they rose to power through their abilities. '卫青' and '霍去病' are Wei Qing and Huo Qubing, famous generals, '皆爱幸' means they were all favored, '然' means but, '亦以功能自进' means they rose to power through their abilities.

  • 寿见宫辄屏御者，托以言事，因与私焉 - Describes He Jin’s interactions with Qin Gong, where he would dismiss his attendants under the pretext of discussing state affairs and engage in private conversations.

  • 冀大起第舍，而寿亦对街为宅，殚极土木，互相夸竞 - Describes how He Jin built grand mansions, and He Jin’s brother He Shou also constructed luxurious residences across the street, competing in opulence.
  • 时人谓之木妖 - Metaphorically refers to the excessive construction as a 'wooden monster' or 'wooden demon,' symbolizing the extravagance and potential downfall.
  • 大将军霍光监奴冯子都，有殊色，光爱幸之 - Describes how Feng Zidu, a eunuch of Grand General Huo Guang, was exceptionally beautiful and favored by Huo Guang.

  • 光卒，显寡居，与子都乱 - Describes how after Huo Guang’s death, his wife Huo Xian, who was a widow, had an affair with Feng Zidu.
  • 显广治第室，作乘舆辇，加画绣裀。冯黄金涂韦，絮荐轮 - Describes how Huo Xian decorated her residence lavishly, including embroidered cushions and gold-painted carriages.
  • 侍婢以五彩丝挽显及子都，游戏第中 - Describes how Huo Xian’s maidservants played with her and Feng Zidu using colorful threads in her residence.
  • 谚云：“堂中无俊仆，心是好人家。”信然 - Proverb suggesting that a good family should have capable servants, which is indeed true.
  • 或言子孟不学无术，此其一征 - Suggests that some people say Zimei (He Jin’s father) lacks education and skill, which is evidenced by this incident.
  • 然则孔光号为名儒，何以献媚董贤也！ - Contrasts He Jin’s father with Kong Guang, a famous scholar, questioning why Kong Guang would flatter Dong Xian.

'空空色色' is an alliterative expression meaning 'emptiness and colorlessness,' suggesting a void or absence. The entire phrase is a rhetorical question: 'Where is the beauty and color now?' It conveys a sense of loss or transience.

'苦相思' means 'suffering from longing' or 'bitter yearning.' The phrase may be a poetic way of expressing that the woman has tattooed or marked her arm with a symbol of her sorrowful longing.

  • 长物有时乎不可暴 - Means 'one's possessions or property may sometimes not be wasted' or 'one's belongings may sometimes not be squandered.'

[1541]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 三春桃李本无言 - Literally 'In the three springs, peach and plum trees are naturally silent,' implying that these trees are inherently beautiful without needing to speak. This is a metaphor for natural beauty and inherent worth.
  • 苦被伤残鸟雀喧 - Literally 'Sadly, they are troubled by the noisy birds.' This suggests that the natural beauty of the trees is marred by the disruptive presence of birds.
  • 借问东邻效西子 - Literally 'Asking the east neighbor to emulate the west lady.' This is a metaphorical way of asking someone to imitate a beautiful or virtuous person.
  • 何如郭素学王轩 - Literally 'How about Guo Su learning from Wang Xuan?' This is another metaphorical reference, comparing Guo Su to Wang Xuan, who is implied to be a model of virtue or beauty.
  • 或言王轩乃吴王后身也 - Literally 'Some say that Wang Xuan is the reincarnation of Wu Wang.' This introduces a mythological or legendary element, suggesting that Wang Xuan has a divine or historical significance.
  • 安知刘导又非王轩之后身乎 - Literally 'How can we know that Liu Dao is not the reincarnation of Wang Xuan?' This extends the idea of reincarnation to Liu Dao, further emphasizing the mystical or legendary nature of the characters.
  • 邪鬼假托 - Literally 'Evil spirits pretending.' This suggests that the events or descriptions might be fabricated or influenced by supernatural forces.
  • 未必真也 - Literally 'It is not necessarily true.' This indicates skepticism about the authenticity of the story.
  • 政和改元，七月之望 - Literally 'In the first year of Zhenghe, on the full moon of July.' This provides a specific historical context, indicating the time period when the events took place.
  • 士人杨彦采，陆升之 - Literally 'Scholars Yang Yanci and Lu Shengzhi.' These are proper names of the individuals involved in the story.
  • 载酒出游莲塘 - Literally 'Carrying wine to tour the lotus pond.' This describes the activity of the scholars.
  • 舟回日夕，夜泊横桥下 - Literally 'As the boat returned at dusk, they anchored under the horizontal bridge at night.' This sets the scene for the evening encounter.
  • 月色明霁 - Literally 'The moonlight was bright and clear.' This describes the beautiful night sky.
  • 酒各半酲 - Literally 'Each had half-drunk.' This indicates that the scholars were slightly intoxicated.
  • 闻邻船有琵琶声 - Literally 'They heard the sound of a pipa from a neighboring boat.' This introduces the musical element of the story.
  • 意其歌姬舟也 - Literally 'They thought it was a boat of a singing girl.' This suggests that the scholars assumed the source of the music was a courtesan.
  • 见灯下一姬，自弄弦索 - Literally 'They saw a lady under a lamp, playing her strings herself.' This describes the woman they encountered.
  • 二人径往见之 - Literally 'The two men went straight to see her.' This shows their curiosity and interest.
  • 询其所由 - Literally 'They asked her about her background.' This indicates that the scholars inquired about the woman's story.
  • 答曰：“妾大都乐籍供奉女也。” - Literally 'She replied, 'I am a lady from the entertainment quarters of the capital.' This explains her background.
  • 从人来游江南 - Literally 'I came to tour Jiangnan with my companions.' This provides more context about her journey.
  • 值彼往云间收布 - Literally 'Just as he went to Yunjian to collect cloth.' This explains why she was alone.
  • 妾独处此候之，尚未回也 - Literally 'I am waiting for him here, and he has not returned yet.' This explains her current situation.
  • 二人命取舟中餕馀，肴核 - Literally 'The two men ordered some leftover food from their boat.' This shows their hospitality.
  • 就灯下同酌 - Literally 'They drank together under the lamp.' This describes the intimate setting.
  • 姬举止闲雅，姿色媚丽 - Literally 'The lady's demeanor was leisurely and elegant, her appearance charming and beautiful.' This describes her character and appearance.
  • 二人情动于中，稍挑谑之 - Literally 'The two men were moved by her, and they began to tease her slightly.' This shows their growing interest and playful interaction.
  • 姬亦不以为嫌 - Literally 'She did not find it objectionable.' This indicates her willingness to engage.
  • 求其歌以侑觞 - Literally 'They asked her to sing to accompany their drinking.' This shows their desire for entertainment.
  • 二人强之，乃曰：“近日游访西子陈迹，得古歌数首，敢奉清尘，不讶为荷。” - Literally 'The two men insisted, and she said, 'Recently, I visited the traces of Xi Shi and obtained several ancient songs. I dare to offer them to you, and I hope you will not be surprised.' This explains why she offered to sing.
  • 凡一歌，侑饮一觞 - Literally 'For each song, they drank one cup of wine.' This describes the ritual of their interaction.
  • 歌曰 - Literally 'The song goes:' This introduces the actual lyrics of the song.

[1553]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 及夜 - By night or at night, indicating the time of the event.
  • 引浚前行 - Led Jun forward; *引* means to lead or guide, *前行* means to go forward.
  • 可数里而至 - Could go several miles and arrive; a classical way of expressing distance traveled.
  • 有青衣数辈，秉烛迎之 - There were several people in green robes, holding candles to welcome them; *青衣* refers to servants or attendants, *数辈* means several people, *秉烛* is 'holding candles,' and *迎之* is 'to welcome them.'
  • 遂延入内室 - Then they invited them into the inner chamber; *延* means to invite or lead in, *内室* is the inner room or private chamber.
  • 与幼芳环坐 - Sat in a circle with Youfang; *环坐* means to sit in a circle.
  • 孔家娘子相邻 - The wife of the Kong family lives nearby; *娘子* is a term for wife or mistress, *相邻* means 'lives nearby.'
  • 使邀之 - Sent someone to invite her; *使* is 'to send,' and *邀之* is 'to invite her.'
  • 今夕偶有佳宾相访 - Tonight, by chance, there is a distinguished guest visiting; *佳宾* is a distinguished guest, *相访* is 'to visit.'
  • 愿因倾觞，以解烦愤 - We wish to pour out cups of wine to relieve our distress; *倾觞* is 'to pour wine,' *解烦愤* is 'to relieve distress or anger.'
  • 少顷而至 - A short while later, she arrived; *少顷* is a short time later.
  • 亦多说陈朝故事 - She also spoke at length about stories from the Chen dynasty; *亦* is 'also,' *多说* is 'spoke at length,' *故事* is 'stories' or 'tales.'
  • 浚因起白曰 - Jun then arose and said; *起白* is 'to rise and speak' or 'to address.'
  • 不审夫人复何姓第 - I do not know, Madam, what your surname and rank are; *不审* is 'I do not know,' *复何* is 'what,' *姓第* is 'surname and rank' or 'family name and status.'
  • 颇贮疑讶 - I have some doubts and suspicions; *颇* is 'somewhat,' *贮* is 'to hold,' *疑讶* is 'doubts and suspicions.'
  • 某即陈朝张贵妃 - I am the Empress Consort Zhang of the Chen dynasty; *某* is a humble way to say 'I,' *即* is 'is,' *贵妃* is 'Empress Consort.'
  • 彼即孔贵嫔 - She is Lady Kong, the Noble Consort; *彼* is 'she,' *贵嫔* is 'Noble Consort.'
  • 谬当后主采顾 - I mistakenly was favored by the later emperor; *谬当* is 'mistakenly held the position of,' *采顾* is 'favored or favored by.'
  • 宠幸之礼，有过妃嫔 - The favor and honor given to me surpassed that of the other concubines; *宠幸之礼* is 'the favor and honors of being favored,' *有过妃嫔* is 'surpassed that of the other concubines.'
  • 不幸国亡，为杨广所杀 - Unfortunately, the country fell, and I was killed by Yang Guang; *不幸* is 'unfortunately,' *为...所杀* is 'was killed by.'
  • 然此贼不仁何甚乎 - But how cruel is this villain!; *然* is 'but,' *贼* is 'villain' or 'traitor,' *不仁* is 'unrighteous' or 'cruel,' *何甚乎* is 'how extreme is this?'
  • 刘禅孙皓，岂无嫔御 - Did Liu Chan and Sun Hao not have concubines?; *刘禅* is Liu Chan (a later emperor of Shu), *孙皓* is Sun Hao (the last emperor of Wu), *嫔御* is 'concubines' or 'attendants.'
  • 独有斯人，行此冤暴 - Only this person committed such unjust cruelty; *独有斯人* is 'only this person,' *行此冤暴* is 'committed this injustice and cruelty.'
  • 且一种亡国，我后主实即风流 - Moreover, in one case of a fallen kingdom, my later emperor was indeed a man of elegance and charm; *且* is 'moreover,' *一种亡国* is 'a kind of fallen kingdom,' *实即风流* is 'was indeed a man of elegance and charm.'
  • 诗酒追欢，琴尊取乐而已 - He pursued pleasure through poetry and wine, and found joy in music and wine alone; *诗酒追欢* is 'pursued pleasure through poetry and wine,' *琴尊取乐而已* is 'found joy in music and wine alone.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 陈朝 - The Chen Dynasty (557–589), a short-lived dynasty of southern China during the Southern and Northern Dynasties period.
  • 张贵妃 - Empress Consort Zhang, a historical figure from the Chen Dynasty, known for her beauty and influence over Emperor Chen Shubao.
  • 孔贵嫔 - Lady Kong, a noble consort in the Chen court, likely a close associate of Empress Consort Zhang.
  • 后主 - 'Later Emperor,' referring to Emperor Chen Shubao, the last emperor of the Chen Dynasty, known for his decadence and the fall of the dynasty.
  • 杨广 - Yang Guang, the Emperor of Sui Dynasty, who overthrew the Chen Dynasty and is known for his tyranny.
  • 刘禅 - Liu Chan, the last emperor of the Shu Han state during the Three Kingdoms period, known for his incompetence and the fall of Shu.
  • 孙皓 - Sun Hao, the last emperor of the Wu state during the Three Kingdoms period, known for his cruelty and the fall of Wu.
  • 诗酒追欢，琴尊取乐 - A poetic description of the lifestyle of a decadent emperor, indulging in poetry, wine, music, and leisure.
  • 嫔御 - A term referring to concubines or female attendants in the imperial court, used here to contrast the behavior of Yang Guang with those of other fallen emperors.

[1554]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不似杨广 - Colloquial expression meaning 'not like Yang Guang,' used to draw a contrast with Yang Guang's actions.
  • 西筑长城，东征辽海 - Describes Yang Guang's military campaigns to the west and east.
  • 使天下男冤女旷，父寡子孤 - Hyperbolic expression meaning 'caused men to be wronged and women to be left alone, fathers to be widowed and children to be orphans,' indicating widespread suffering.
  • 途穷广陵，死于匹夫之手 - Phrase meaning 'when he was in a desperate situation in Guangling, he was killed by a commoner,' indicating Yang Guang's downfall.
  • 亦上天降鉴，为我报仇耳 - Phrase meaning 'it was also the will of heaven to punish him and avenge me,' indicating a sense of divine retribution.
  • 浑忘却 - Colloquial expression meaning 'completely forgot,' used to express surprise or disbelief.
  • 何人不欲闻斯言耶 - Rhetorical question meaning 'who wouldn't want to hear these words?' used to emphasize the importance of the statement.
  • 某本江令公家嬖者，后为贵妃侍儿 - Phrase meaning 'I was originally a favored concubine of General Jiang, later a servant of the Empress,' indicating the speaker's background.
  • 国亡之后为隋宫御女 - Phrase meaning 'after the country fell, I became a maid in the Sui palace,' indicating a change in status.
  • 炀帝江都，为侍汤膳者 - Phrase meaning 'Emperor Yang was in Jiangdu, and I served as his attendant,' indicating a specific role.
  • 及兵乱，某入以身蔽帝，遂为所害 - Phrase meaning 'when the army rebelled, I rushed in to shield the emperor and was killed,' indicating a heroic act.
  • 萧后怜某尽忠于主，因使殉葬 - Phrase meaning 'Empress Xiao pitied me for my loyalty to the master and ordered me to be buried with him,' indicating a posthumous honor.
  • 后改葬于雷唐侧，不得从焉 - Phrase meaning 'later, I was reburied next to Lei Tang, but I could not accompany him,' indicating a change in burial location.
  • 时至此谒贵妃耳 - Phrase meaning 'now I come here to pay respects to the Empress,' indicating the speaker's current purpose.
  • 前说尽是闲事，不如命酒，略延曩日之欢耳 - Phrase meaning 'all the previous talk is trivial, let's get some wine and enjoy a bit of the past pleasure,' indicating a shift in conversation.
  • 遂命双鬟持乐器，洽饮 - Phrase meaning 'she then ordered the two maids to bring musical instruments and they all drank together,' indicating a festive atmosphere.
  • 久之，贵妃题诗一章曰 - Phrase meaning 'after a while, the Empress composed a poem,' indicating a poetic moment.
  • 秋草荒台响夜蛩，白杨凋尽减悲风 - Phrase meaning 'autumn grass on the desolate platform echoes with the chirping of night insects, the white poplars have all fallen, reducing the sorrowful wind,' indicating a melancholic scene.
  • 彩笺曾擘斯江惣，绮阁尘清玉树空 - Phrase meaning 'once the colorful letter was torn in this Jiang, the beautiful pavilion is now dusty, and the jade tree is empty,' indicating a sense of loss.
  • 宝阁排云称望仙，五云高艳拥朝天 - Phrase meaning 'the treasure pavilion, rising above the clouds, is called the 'Auspicious Palace,' five clouds of high beauty surround the imperial court,' indicating a grand and divine setting.
  • 清溪犹有当时月，夜照琼花绽荷筵 - Phrase meaning 'the clear stream still has the moon from that time, at night it illuminates the jade flowers blooming on the lotus banquet,' indicating a serene and beautiful scene.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 杨广西筑长城，东征辽海 - Refers to Emperor Yang of Sui's military campaigns to the west and east, which were costly and led to widespread suffering.
  • 途穷广陵，死于匹夫之手 - Refers to Emperor Yang's downfall and death in Guangling, which was a significant event in Sui history.
  • 炀帝江都 - Refers to Emperor Yang's residence in Jiangdu, a city in present-day Yangzhou, Jiangsu Province.

  • 彼官人何得挟伤亡鬼自随 - A metaphorical expression suggesting that the official (俊) is accompanied by a ghost of a deceased person.
  • 不过三月死矣 - Predicts that the official will die within three months.
  • 初尚抵讳 - Means 'initially still denying or refusing to admit.'
  • 比而惊悟 - Means 'later suddenly realizing or becoming aware.'
  • 彼何由知 - Means 'how could he know?'
  • 必有异 - Indicates that there must be something unusual or supernatural.
  • 拉刘访之旅邸 - Means 'to take Liu to visit the inn.'
  • 官员肯寻我耶 - Means 'would the official come to find me?'
  • 不然，几坏性命 - Means 'otherwise, almost ruined my life.'
  • 使俊吞之 - Means 'to make Jun swallow them.'
  • 密窥之 - Means 'to secretly observe it.'
  • 作法摩诃状 - Describes a ritualistic or magical gesture.
  • 一更后 - Refers to the first watch of the night.
  • 闻门外女子哭声 - Means 'to hear the sound of a woman crying outside the door.'
  • 三更乃寂 - Means 'until the third watch when it became silent.'
  • 明旦 - Means 'the next morning.'
  • 戒后勿复往寺中 - Means 'to warn against going back to the temple again.'
  • 诸僧后知其事 - Means 'the monks later learned about the incident.'
  • 寺之左右，素无妖怪之属 - Means 'there were no known ghosts or spirits around the temple.'
  • 惟昔年邵宏渊太尉谪官时，丧一笄女，葬于后墙之外，必此也 - Refers to a specific historical event involving a former official named Shao Hongyuan who was exiled and buried his daughter outside the temple wall.
  • 自是遂常出为僧患，僧甚苦之 - Means 'from then on, the ghost frequently caused problems for the monks, causing them great distress.'
  • 遣仆谐武陵白邵，请改葬 - Means 'to send a servant to Wu Ling to inform Shao and request a reburial.'
  • 邵许之，乃瘗于北门外五里田侧 - Means 'Shao agreed, and the body was reburied five miles north of the city gate.'
  • 复出扰居者，又徙于深山，其鬼始绝 - Means 'it continued to cause trouble for the residents, and they moved to a deep mountain, finally ending the ghost's presence.'

  • 货药人 - Refers to a person who sells medicine, which was a common profession in ancient China.

  • 邵宏渊太尉 - A historical figure, likely a high-ranking official during the Southern Song Dynasty.

  • 不为意 - Means 'not paying attention' or 'not taking notice,' indicating indifference.

  • 特相从 - Means 'especially to follow you,' indicating a specific intention.
  • 谋一宵之欢尔 - Means 'to seek a night of pleasure,' suggesting a romantic or intimate encounter.

[1611]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 何以惊为？” - Rhetorical question meaning 'Why are you surprised?' or 'What is there to be surprised about?'
  • 汝何自知吾？” - Question meaning 'How do you know me?' or 'What makes you think you know me?'
  • 盖适尾其同辈行，得之其口。 - Phrase meaning 'It seems that she heard it from her peers,' indicating that the information came from her friends or acquaintances.
  • 便已迷惑，偕行至家。 - Phrase meaning 'He was already bewildered and walked home with her,' suggesting a state of confusion or fascination.
  • 自钥其户，逮归，不唤翁妪，自启其寝，则女已在室中坐矣。 - Sequence of actions meaning 'He locked his door when he left, and upon returning, he did not call the old man and woman but opened his own room, finding the girl already sitting inside.'
  • 亦不晤其何以先在也。 - Phrase meaning 'He also did not understand how she could have been there before him.'
  • 灯下谛玩之，殊倍媚嫣，新妆浓艳，衣饰亦极鲜华，皆绮罗盛服也。 - Description meaning 'Under the lamp, he carefully observed her, and she appeared even more charming and beautiful, with fresh makeup and bright, colorful clothing, all in fine silk and brocade.'
  • 即从案上取一盒子，启之，中有熟鸡鱼肉之类，及温酒，取共饮食，其肴（肉）犹热也。 - Description meaning 'She immediately took a box from the table, opened it, and found cooked chicken, fish, and meat, along with warm wine, which they shared, and the dishes were still hot.'
  • 啖已，就寝。 - Phrase meaning 'After eating, they went to bed.'
  • 女解衣，内外皆斩然新制，乃与之合，犹处子尔。 - Description meaning 'The girl undressed, revealing new and clean undergarments, and they made love, as if she were still a virgin.'
  • 将黎明，自去，少年固不知其何人也。 - Phrase meaning 'At dawn, she left, and the young man still did not know who she was.'
  • 迨夜复至，与之饮食寝合如昨。 - Phrase meaning 'When night came again, she returned, and they ate, drank, and slept together as before.'
  • 既而无夕不至，久而愈密。 - Phrase meaning 'Afterward, she came every night, and over time, their meetings became more frequent and intimate.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 少年 - Refers to a young man, likely a commoner or a lower-ranking official.
  • 翁妪 - Refers to the old man and woman, likely the parents or guardians of the young man.
  • 爨室 - Refers to the kitchen, where food is prepared.
  • 处子 - Refers to a virgin, emphasizing the girl's purity and innocence.
  • 绮罗盛服 - Refers to fine silk and brocade, indicating the girl's high social status or wealth.
